qualitative_claim: The transcript-derived model alone must be the benchmark.
bullish_rule: Use final baseline bullish signal with no TICK or L2 columns in scoring.
bearish_rule: Use final baseline bearish signal with no TICK or L2 columns in scoring.
required_exported_columns: signal_id; timestamp; instrument; direction; grade; baseline_total_score; baseline_active_modules; edge_level_type; edge_price; entry_reference; stop_reference
causal_timing_rule: Use only closed-bar footprint/order-flow data available at baseline signal time.
expected_edge: Clean benchmark for all enhancements.
failure_mode: Enhanced models may look better only because they reduce frequency or use delayed information.
notes: Preserve as sacred control.

qualitative_claim: TICK extreme at the same reversal location may show broad-market exhaustion.
bullish_rule: Baseline bullish signal gets score boost if TICK is at negative extreme and begins stabilizing by signal/confirmation time.
bearish_rule: Baseline bearish signal gets score boost if TICK is at positive extreme and begins stabilizing by signal/confirmation time.
required_exported_columns: tick_value_at_signal; tick_percentile; tick_extreme_flag; tick_slope_pre_signal; tick_recovery_at_confirm; baseline_signal_id
causal_timing_rule: If used for entry score, TICK must be known at or before confirmed signal; recovery after signal is delayed-confirmation only.
expected_edge: Better selectivity for broad-market exhaustion reversals.
failure_mode: Extreme TICK can persist in trend days and make fades worse.
notes: Test as soft boost before veto.

qualitative_claim: TICK failing to confirm a new price extreme may strengthen reversal.
bullish_rule: Price makes lower low but TICK makes higher low or less extreme reading; boost long baseline signal.
bearish_rule: Price makes higher high but TICK makes lower high or less extreme reading; boost short baseline signal.
causal_timing_rule: Use TICK values up to the price extreme and confirmed signal; no future recovery unless delayed confirmation.
expected_edge: Filters local footprint signals that align with broader internals divergence.
failure_mode: TICK divergence can appear repeatedly during strong trend and fail.
notes: Useful for ES/NQ/YM index products; weaker for non-index futures.

qualitative_claim: TICK recovery after a sweep can confirm that broad selling/buying pressure is reversing.
bullish_rule: After downside sweep/reclaim, TICK recovers from negative extreme before entry or delayed confirmation.
bearish_rule: After upside sweep/rejection, TICK rolls over from positive extreme before entry or delayed confirmation.
causal_timing_rule: If recovery occurs after baseline signal, entry timestamp must be delayed to recovery confirmation.
expected_edge: May reduce failed sweep fades.
failure_mode: Delayed confirmation may worsen R and miss best entries.
notes: Compare immediate baseline entry vs TICK-delayed entry.

qualitative_claim: TICK confirming the breakout direction may veto countertrend reversals.
bullish_rule: Veto long if price is making lows and TICK is also making fresh negative extremes without recovery.
bearish_rule: Veto short if price is making highs and TICK is also making fresh positive extremes without rollover.
causal_timing_rule: Veto uses only TICK state at signal/confirmation time.
expected_edge: Cuts fades during broad-market continuation.
failure_mode: Can remove strong local reversals that lead TICK recovery.
notes: Hard veto only after soft score proves value.

qualitative_claim: Index top-of-book confirming broad pressure may help separate local turn from market-wide push.
bullish_rule: Boost long if related index top-of-book pressure stops making lower bids/offers near baseline long.
bearish_rule: Boost short if related index top-of-book pressure stops making higher bids/offers near baseline short.
required_exported_columns: index_bid; index_ask; index_mid; index_spread; index_mid_slope; index_quote_velocity; baseline_signal_id
causal_timing_rule: Use quote state at/before signal; future index move is outcome only.
expected_edge: Adds broad-market confirmation beyond local contract footprint.
failure_mode: Index feed may be noisy or misaligned; correlation varies by instrument.
notes: Use for ES/NQ/YM first.

qualitative_claim: Depth imbalance near the reversal edge may confirm passive defense.
bullish_rule: At support/low, bid depth imbalance within 1/3/5/10 levels supports long baseline signal.
bearish_rule: At resistance/high, ask depth imbalance within 1/3/5/10 levels supports short baseline signal.
required_exported_columns: l2_bid_depth_1; l2_ask_depth_1; l2_bid_depth_3; l2_ask_depth_3; l2_bid_depth_5; l2_ask_depth_5; l2_bid_depth_10; l2_ask_depth_10; l2_depth_imbalance_*; edge_price
causal_timing_rule: Snapshot must be from before or at signal confirmation, not after reversal starts.
expected_edge: Improves absorption scoring with actual resting liquidity context.
failure_mode: Displayed depth can spoof/pull; static imbalance may not hold.
notes: First L2 feature to test as soft score.

qualitative_claim: Passive replenishment after aggressive trades is stronger absorption evidence than footprint alone.
bullish_rule: After aggressive selling at low, bid depth replenishes at/behind edge while price fails lower; confirm long.
bearish_rule: After aggressive buying at high, ask depth replenishes at/behind edge while price fails higher; confirm short.
required_exported_columns: aggressive_trade_volume_at_edge; l2_bid_replenish_rate; l2_ask_replenish_rate; l2_depth_before_edge; l2_depth_after_trade; edge_price
causal_timing_rule: If replenishment is measured after aggressive trade but before entry/confirmation, it is valid; post-entry replenishment is outcome.
expected_edge: More causal absorption confirmation.
failure_mode: Requires precise time alignment between trades and book updates.
notes: Highest-value L2 idea; hardest to implement cleanly.

qualitative_claim: Book flipping after a sweep may confirm failed acceptance.
bullish_rule: After downside sweep, book pressure flips from ask/sell pressure to bid support before/during reclaim; long.
bearish_rule: After upside sweep, book pressure flips from bid/buy pressure to ask supply before/during rejection; short.
required_exported_columns: l2_book_pressure_pre_sweep; l2_book_pressure_at_sweep; l2_book_pressure_post_sweep; sweep_flag; reclaim_flag; book_flip_flag
causal_timing_rule: If post-sweep flip is required, entry must be delayed until flip timestamp.
expected_edge: Filters real sweeps from continuation breaks.
failure_mode: Book can flip after most of move; delayed entries may be worse.
notes: Test immediate vs delayed book-flip confirmation.

qualitative_claim: If visible liquidity disappears and market accepts through the level, do not fade.
bullish_rule: Veto long after downside break if bid depth pulls and ask pressure remains dominant below level.
bearish_rule: Veto short after upside break if ask depth pulls and bid pressure remains dominant above level.
causal_timing_rule: Veto uses book state at/before baseline confirmation; if measured later, label delayed veto.
expected_edge: Avoids fading real breaks where passive side gives up.
failure_mode: Hard veto may block reversals caused by liquidity vacuum snaps.
notes: Test soft penalty before hard veto.

qualitative_claim: L2 can improve entry timing around a baseline footprint signal.
bullish_rule: For baseline long, enter on first book-pressure flip/replenishment after confirmed footprint edge instead of fixed next-bar trigger.
bearish_rule: For baseline short, enter on first ask-pressure flip/replenishment after confirmed footprint edge instead of fixed next-bar trigger.
required_exported_columns: baseline_signal_timestamp; l2_book_pressure; l2_microprice; l2_replenish_rate; l2_timing_trigger_timestamp; entry_reference
causal_timing_rule: Timing trigger must occur after baseline signal and before hypothetical entry; compare delayed fill honestly.
expected_edge: May reduce MAE and improve entry quality.
failure_mode: May miss fast reversals or chase after move starts.
notes: Separate from confirmation-only tests.

qualitative_claim: Microprice/book pressure diverging from price can reveal hidden book support/supply.
bullish_rule: Price makes lower low while microprice stops falling or rises; boost long signal.
bearish_rule: Price makes higher high while microprice stops rising or falls; boost short signal.
causal_timing_rule: Use microprice up to signal/confirmation time only.
expected_edge: Earlier warning than footprint close.
failure_mode: Microprice noisy in thin/fast books; can overfit.
notes: Soft score only initially.

qualitative_claim: Unstable quotes/spread can make footprint reversal signals unreliable.
bullish_rule: Veto or downscore long if spread widens and quote churn is extreme at signal.
bearish_rule: Veto or downscore short if spread widens and quote churn is extreme at signal.
causal_timing_rule: Use quote state at/before signal only.
expected_edge: Avoids bad fills and fake liquidity signals.
failure_mode: May remove good event-driven reversals.
notes: Start as risk penalty, not hard veto.

qualitative_claim: Depth can confirm absorption and invalidate quickly if passive side vanishes.
bullish_rule: Confirm long if bid replenishes; invalidate if bid pulls and price accepts below edge.
bearish_rule: Confirm short if ask replenishes; invalidate if ask pulls and price accepts above edge.
causal_timing_rule: Confirmation/invalidation timestamps must be explicit; no post-hoc use.
expected_edge: May reduce losers faster.
failure_mode: Can overreact to normal book flicker.
notes: Test after soft L2 score is proven.

qualitative_claim: Best reversals may show local passive defense plus broad-market exhaustion.
bullish_rule: Long if baseline signal plus TICK negative extreme/divergence plus bid-depth support/replenishment.
bearish_rule: Short if baseline signal plus TICK positive extreme/divergence plus ask-depth support/replenishment.
causal_timing_rule: All features must be available at signal or delayed confirmation timestamp.
expected_edge: Highest confluence version without brittle veto.
failure_mode: May reduce frequency too far; features may be redundant.
notes: Full soft model before full hard model.

qualitative_claim: Hard veto version tests whether internals/depth should block weak baseline signals.
bullish_rule: Long baseline is vetoed if TICK confirms downside and bid depth pulls/does not replenish.
bearish_rule: Short baseline is vetoed if TICK confirms upside and ask depth pulls/does not replenish.
causal_timing_rule: Veto must occur at/before entry timestamp.
expected_edge: Potential large false-positive reduction.
failure_mode: Can delete too many winners and create hindsight filters.
notes: Only after soft model shows monotonic value.

qualitative_claim: Strict A+ tests quality over frequency.
bullish_rule: Only A+ baseline/enhanced signals with no hard caps and strongest confirmation are tradable.
bearish_rule: Only A+ baseline/enhanced signals with no hard caps and strongest confirmation are tradable.
causal_timing_rule: Grade known at signal/confirmation time only.
expected_edge: Higher expectancy, lower frequency.
failure_mode: Too few trades, missed A/B winners.
notes: Compare against broad A/B model.

qualitative_claim: Broader A/B model may capture more total expectancy despite lower quality.
bullish_rule: Allow A+ and A/B signals with smaller size or wider management rules.
bearish_rule: Allow A+ and A/B signals with smaller size or wider management rules.
causal_timing_rule: Grade known at signal/confirmation time only.
expected_edge: Higher sample size and possibly better total PnL.
failure_mode: More false positives and drawdown.
notes: Must compare expectancy and drawdown, not win rate alone.

qualitative_claim: Resting liquidity at prior high/low may act as a magnet or rejection point.
bullish_rule: At prior low/support, visible bid liquidity resting/replenishing strengthens long rejection.
bearish_rule: At prior high/resistance, visible ask liquidity resting/replenishing strengthens short rejection.
causal_timing_rule: Snapshot before/at signal only; later resting liquidity is outcome.
expected_edge: Improves level quality with real book context.
failure_mode: Displayed liquidity may pull before fill.
notes: Treat as level-quality enhancer.

qualitative_claim: Depth behavior around sweep distinguishes stop run from true breakout.
bullish_rule: Before/during downside sweep, bid liquidity absorbs/replenishes and book flips after reclaim; long.
bearish_rule: Before/during upside sweep, ask liquidity absorbs/replenishes and book flips after rejection; short.
causal_timing_rule: If after-sweep depth is used, entry must be delayed or labeled confirmation model.
expected_edge: More precise failed-sweep diagnosis.
failure_mode: Complex and easy to leak future info.
notes: Use event windows with strict timestamps.
